qa_pairs.question: What steps must a Principal Investigator (PI) take to renew their Alliance account and manage their sponsored users?
qa_pairs.answer: Principal Investigators (PIs) must update their contact information and Field of Research on the account renewal form, complete a User Satisfaction Survey, and update the status of their sponsored roles (e.g., students, postdocs). University faculty who sponsor other users must specifically confirm whether those accounts are continuing or have ended. Once all information is updated and confirmed, the PI must click the 'REQUEST ACCOUNT RENEWAL' button on the left side of the form to initiate the renewal.

qa_pairs.question: If a Principal Investigator (PI) with a faculty role moves to a different Alliance institution, will users sponsored by their old role be automatically transferred to their new role?
qa_pairs.answer: No, users sponsored by the PI's old faculty role will not be automatically transferred to their new role. Any user the PI wishes to sponsor with their new faculty role will have to apply for a new role sponsored by that new position. If the PI has a RAC allocation, sponsored users with the old role will lose access to the associated Resource Allocation Project (RAP) and may need to be re-added to the RAP once their new roles are activated.

qa_pairs.question: Why might a sponsored Alliance account remain deactivated on CCDB even after the sponsored user has completed their renewal?
qa_pairs.answer: A sponsored Alliance account may remain deactivated even after the sponsored user completes their renewal because their sponsor has not yet renewed or reactivated their own account. The sponsored role will only be activated once the sponsor renews their account and confirms that the sponsored role is continuing.
